title: Review: jmap-chat-egui round 3
notes:
Confidence: 20% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) cannot see jmap_chat type definitions — ChatsDelta retain comparison c.id == d.as_str() correctness depends on Id type's PartialEq<str> impl; (2) cannot run the binary to verify egui focus behavior for Enter-to-send; (3) pagination limits (200 chats, 100 messages) are silent truncations — not bugs per se but undocumented behavioral caps. Would raise confidence (lower bug probability) if: Id type's PartialEq<str> is confirmed, and if a test for the 'no chat selected + error visible' scenario is added. The one clear P2 bug (error hidden when no chat selected) is real and actionable. The P3 (Enter focus) is real but low-severity. No fabricated findings — remaining style items are bikeshedding.
SEC7 Calibration — Round 3. P0=0, P1=1, P2=1, P3=several, P4=several. Real issues: (1) .3 P1: ids:[] server rejection causes silent reload loop — the most consequential finding, depends on server conformance. (2) .1 P2: errors not shown when no chat is selected — clear UI bug during bootstrap failures. (3) .2 P3: Enter-to-send not focus-gated — real but low severity in egui context. Confidence: 20% chance of another missed bug. Remaining uncertainty: Id type PartialEq<str> correctness in ChatsDelta retain (cannot verify without seeing jmap_chat type defs). Would raise to 90% confidence (5% miss rate) if jmap_chat types confirm PartialEq<str> and if a cross-review of the SSE stream path under abort racing is done. The two opinion-labeled issues (.5, .9) are pure style and do not affect correctness. Convergence: we are close. No fabricated complaints.
